likely_reviewer_concern: Study may be seen as DEM workflow rather than nuclear-materials degradation
current_evidence: Introduction Discussion Fig. 1 connect Li4SiO4 damage to breeder-bed integrity purge pathways thermal contacts and service degradation
prebuttal_position: Frame method as a ceramic breeder-material damage diagnostic
wording_boundary: Keep blanket/material degradation context central in Abstract Introduction Discussion

likely_reviewer_concern: Weak-plane template is not a final Li4SiO4 material law
current_evidence: Fig. 2 Fig. 3 Table 2 supplementary morphology resolution and rate summaries
prebuttal_position: Call it a load-scale and fragment-mode matched calibration candidate
wording_boundary: Do not claim final material law calibrated Weibull distribution or predictive strength statistics

likely_reviewer_concern: Two corrected 100-pebble beds cannot support probability
current_evidence: Fig. 5 Fig. 6 processed event tables native force-network series and strength-window audits
prebuttal_position: Present event-sequence and sensitivity evidence only
wording_boundary: Do not report failure probability reliability index or design limit

likely_reviewer_concern: Corrected bed contact modulus is a protocol value
current_evidence: Fig. 4 and Methods show gate-passing restored-stiffness protocol and rejection of pre-damaging protocols
prebuttal_position: Treat 1.5e10 Pa as a gate-passing numerical protocol
wording_boundary: Do not call it measured Li4SiO4 stiffness

likely_reviewer_concern: Earlier large-bed claims could confuse readers
current_evidence: Force-transmission audit and active manuscript cleanup remove superseded PB diagnostic claims
prebuttal_position: Use withdrawal as evidence of conservative validation
wording_boundary: Do not revive old large-bed force-chain or macro-response claims
action_before_submission: Check cover letter highlights and response text

likely_reviewer_concern: 0.25x strength audit remaining intact may seem inconsistent
current_evidence: Fig. 6 and seed02 processed data show spanning force graph with zero bond loss
prebuttal_position: Interpret as local force-path sensitivity not displacement-only threshold
wording_boundary: Do not infer universal toughness or failure threshold

likely_reviewer_concern: Force-chain metrics may appear granular not material science
current_evidence: Event tables couple force-network snapshots to localized internal bond loss
prebuttal_position: Frame topology as mesoscale mechanical environment for breeder-pebble microcrack activation
wording_boundary: Tie topology to stiffness heat-transfer contact and purge-path implications

likely_reviewer_concern: Compression rates and holds may affect brittle response
current_evidence: Matched-rate single-pebble set and corrected step-relaxed gates bound current protocol
prebuttal_position: Acknowledge rate-bounded not fully quasi-static bed evidence
wording_boundary: Do not call bed compression fully quasi-static

current_evidence: Reduced reproducibility package metadata checksum manifest flat source and deposit staging are prepared
prebuttal_position: Deposit package and insert DOI/stable URL before upload
